creator: disappearedng@gmail.com
text:
startx fails to start x after a couple of successful log ins and outs.

I am currently having problems with kde which does not allow me to prompts me an error message and boots me out back to console. After a few successful startx attempts, startx fails with the following message

[code]
xauth:  creating new authority file /home/disappearedng/.serverauth.1313
xauth: (argv):1:  bad display name "home.lan" in "list" command
xauth: (stdin):1:  bad display name "home.lan" in "add" command


X.Org X Server 1.4.0
Release Date: 5 September 2007
X Protocol Version 11, Revision 0
Build Operating System: FreeBSD 7.0-RELEASE i386
Current Operating System: FreeBSD home.lan  7.0-RELEASE FreeBSD 7.0-RELEASE #5:
Sun Jun 29 18:22:24 UTC 2008     root@.lan:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/miuky1 i386
Build Date: 13 February 2008  05:50:12PM

        Before reporting problems, check http://wiki.x.org
        to make sure that you have the latest version.
Module Loader present
Markers: (--) probed, (**) from config file, (==) default setting,
        (++) from command line, (!!) notice, (II) informational,
        (WW) warning, (EE) error, (NI) not implemented, (??) unknown.
(==) Log file: "/var/log/Xorg.0.log", Time: Tue Jul 15 22:24:49 2008
(==) Using config file: "/etc/X11/xorg.conf"
(EE) I810(0): V_BIOS address 0x0 out of range
(EE) I810(0): VBE initialization failed.
(EE) Screen(s) found, but none have a usable configuration.

Fatal server error:
no screens found
XIO:  fatal IO error 53 (Software caused connection abort) on X server ":0.0"
      after 0 requests (0 known processed) with 0 events remaining.
xauth: (argv):1:  bad display name "home.lan" in "remove" command
[/code]

However, after a shutdown everything is fine again until this process repeats.

How-To-Repeat: Restart, startx a few times, terminate all these startx and startx again
